results.alt: By attaching fluorescent tags to specific proteins or structures, scientists can illuminate the inner workings of living cells in striking colors. This technique reveals the dynamic processes of life as they happen in real time. Fluorescence Microscopy

By scanning the surface of a sample with a focused beam of electrons, we can capture incredibly detailed, three-dimensional topological images. This allows us to see structures at nanometer resolution, far beyond the limits of optical microscopes. Scanning Electron Microscopy
